PE buyers don't just pay for revenue.
They pay for a business that runs without the founder.

The Bottom Line — Upfront

The math before anything else.

The goal of this engagement is to transition Bear Door Services from an owner-dependent operation into a systemized, documented asset — the kind that commands a premium exit multiple when you go to market.

1–2×
Where owner-dependent
businesses sell today
3–5×
Where systemized,
PE-ready businesses sell
$1M–$3M
Difference in exit value
at Bear Door's current revenue pace
The revenue is already there. The gap is the infrastructure between what Bear Door is today and what a PE buyer will pay a premium for. This document is the plan to close that gap.
01 — Where You Are

You've built something exceptional.

Bear Door dominates Uniontown — #1 on LSA, more reviews than every competitor, margins 3–4× the industry average, on pace for $1M in revenue. Two trucks. Year 2. Most garage door companies spend their entire careers trying to reach this.

#1
LSA ranking, local market
3–4×
competitor pricing — still fully booked
$1M
revenue pace, Year 2
"You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems."
— James Clear, Atomic Habits

Bear Door's biggest risk right now has nothing to do with revenue. It's operational dependency. If Michael takes a week off, gets sick, or steps back — what happens to the business? When a PE firm opens the hood for due diligence, those are the exact questions they ask:

📋

"Show us your training manual."

If the answer is "it's in Michael's head," the multiple drops. Buyers pay for businesses that can be handed off.

📊

"Break out your KPIs by source."

Close rate by lead channel, margin per job type, time-to-cash — if Phil can't pull a dashboard, buyers discount the ask.

📍

"How do you scale to Pittsburgh?"

If the answer is "Michael trains them himself," that's not a scalable business. That's a job that happens to generate revenue.

02 — Where You're Going

What we build toward, together.

Not vague "AI transformation." Specific, operational infrastructure that makes Bear Door independently valuable — systems a PE buyer can verify, trust, and pay a premium for.

Outcome 01

A business Michael can step back from

Leads handled, jobs dispatched, follow-ups sent, reviews requested — automated. Michael sets the rules once. The system runs them every day.

Outcome 02

KPI visibility Phil can act on

Lead source performance, job type margins, time-to-cash, close rates — exactly what Phil described. Live. Exportable. Shareable with buyers.

Outcome 03

Due diligence Phil will be proud to show

SOPs documented, onboarding systemized, multi-location infrastructure in place — everything a PE firm looks for, ready before the first meeting.

03 — The Roadmap

A Foundation Sprint, then ongoing build.

Phase 1 is a focused, fixed-scope sprint. After that, each month we build on the foundation — adding the reporting, marketing, documentation, and polish that changes what Bear Door is worth at exit.

Phase 1

Foundation Sprint

~60 days · fixed scope
Risk-Free Acceleration Sprint

GHL + Jobber Integration

Jobber keeps handling field ops. GoHighLevel connects via custom API and webhooks — syncing leads, jobs, and customer data in real time. One source of truth without rebuilding what's working.

AI Receptionist + Lead Follow-Up

New inquiry → AI responds in under 60 seconds, qualifies the lead, routes to schedule. Missed call → auto-text immediately. Michael stops being the first point of contact for every incoming lead.

iPad Service Catalog

Mobile web app (Vercel-hosted, connected to GHL). Tap a category on-site — see pricing, options, photos. Techs close higher-ticket jobs without calling the office or negotiating on price.

Training & Onboarding Portal

SOPs, training videos, milestone checklists, role-based tracks — in one private portal. New hires certify before touching a customer. Pittsburgh expansion is a hiring problem, not a training crisis.

By Day 60, four fully functional MVPs are live in your business. Every system, account, and login is yours to keep from day one — the build belongs to you, not us.
Phase 2

Growth & Systems

Ongoing monthly build

Phil's KPI Dashboards

Lead source attribution (LSA / generic calls / Meta / referrals / social). Job type KPIs: avg price, margin %, time from first contact to payment collected. Live, web-based, shareable URL for any buyer conversation.

Google + Facebook Ad Management

Amplify what's working in LSA. Layer in Google Ads and Facebook campaigns as content scales. One team managing the full funnel — not three separate vendors each billing separately.

SEO + AI Content for beardoorservices.com

Consistent content targeting financing, new door installs, and the Pittsburgh market — building domain authority before the expansion opens.

Hiring + Scheduling Infrastructure

Systems to hire and onboard a customer service rep so Michael exits the phone-answering role entirely. Hiring funnel, onboarding track, scheduling playbook — built into GHL and the training portal.

Phase 3

Exit-Ready

As you approach your window

Full SOP Library

Every process documented, version-controlled, organized. When a PE firm asks "how do you do X?" — the answer is already written, accessible to anyone in the company.

Executive Dashboard — Buyer-Ready

A polished, live dashboard Phil can put in front of any buyer: revenue per job, CAC, LTV, review velocity, close rate by source — branded, real-time, exportable.

Multi-Location GHL Setup

Pittsburgh added as a separate GHL workspace — same systems, same reporting, same playbook from day one. No rebuild when you expand.

Valuation Readiness Audit

Before you go to market, we audit every system against what PE buyers flag in diligence. Fix the gaps before the first meeting — not during it.

05 — How We Build

The technical architecture.

Phil asked: what do you build with? Here's the exact stack. Nothing proprietary, nothing locked to us. You own every subscription, every account, every line of code we write for you.

Jobber

Field Ops + Scheduling

You keep Jobber. It handles scheduling, dispatch, field invoicing, and client intake — what it already does well. We don't rebuild what's working.

  • Dispatch
  • Field invoicing
  • Client intake
custom API + webhooks · real-time sync
GoHighLevel

Automation Engine + Data Hub

GHL is the brain. Every lead, every automation, every customer interaction routes through here. ~$100–200/mo — your subscription, your account, forever.

  • Lead routing
  • AI workflows
  • CRM data
  • Automations

deploy + host
Vercel

Your Web Apps

06 — Foundation Sprint in Detail

Four systems. Sixty days. Yours to keep.

The sprint delivers four fully functional MVPs — live and operational in your business within ~60 days. Each one is refined continuously in the months that follow. Every system lives in your accounts from day one.

GHL + Jobber Integration

Jobber runs field ops. GHL connects via custom webhooks — syncing customer data, job status, and lead info in real time. Everything flows without manual entry or double-entry.

↗ Ongoing: KPI dashboards, Pittsburgh location, advanced dispatching

AI Receptionist + Lead Follow-Up

New inquiry → AI responds in under 60 seconds, qualifies the lead, routes to schedule. Missed call → auto-text immediately. Michael exits the role of first responder to every incoming contact.

↗ Ongoing: Nurture sequences, review automation, re-engagement

iPad Service Catalog

Mobile web app (HTML/CSS, Vercel-hosted, GHL-connected). Tap a category on-site — options, pricing, photos. Closes higher-ticket jobs without negotiating on price or calling the office.

↗ Ongoing: Digital quote signing, financing calculator, door visualizer

Training & Onboarding Portal

Vercel-hosted portal with SOPs, training videos, milestone checklists, role-based tracks. New hires certify before touching a customer. Pittsburgh expansion is a hiring problem — not a training crisis.

↗ Ongoing: Full SOP library, manager dashboard, diligence package

You own everything we build. Every GHL account, every Vercel app, every webhook — cancel and it all stays yours.

07 — Platform Cost Transparency

Every tool you'll need. What it costs.

We set up every subscription in your name. You own these accounts outright. Below is the full picture of what running these systems costs — no surprises.

Platform Est. Monthly Cost You Own It What It Does
Jobber Already paying ✓ Field ops, scheduling, field invoicing — keep as-is
GoHighLevel $97–$297 / mo ✓ CRM, automation engine, data hub, reporting
GHL AI + SMS Credits ~$20–$50 / mo ✓ AI receptionist, automated texts, call handling
Vercel Hosting $0–$20 / mo ✓ Hosts the catalog, training portal, and KPI dashboard
Google Ads Budget Set by you ✓ We manage the campaigns; you control the spend
Facebook Ads Budget Set by you ✓ We manage the campaigns; you control the spend
Platform Total (est.) ~$140–$370 / mo ✓ All yours — cancel Disruptors and keep everything
Ad spend is separate from platform costs and separate from the monthly build fee — and always controlled by you, not us.
08 — Monthly Systems Build

What each month funds.

A fractional Chief AI Officer who shows up every week, builds what your business needs next, and never hands you off to someone else.

  • Weekly Strategy Call — Kimball, directly 30 min to 1 hr, every week. What's the bottleneck right now? That's what we build next. No account managers, no relay, no "I'll check with the team."
  • Ongoing GHL Builds + Automation Refinements CRM improvements, new workflows, dispatching logic, reporting dashboards — continuous improvement, every month, building on the last.
  • SEO + AI Content for beardoorservices.com Consistent content targeting garage door financing, new door installs, and the Pittsburgh market — building authority before the expansion opens.
  • Full-Funnel Marketing — Ads + AI Content Google Ads and Facebook campaigns amplify what's already working in LSA. AI-assisted content (1–2 posts/week: before/afters, tips, reviews) publishes automatically. One team, one bill, full funnel covered.
  • AI Workflow Expansion — New System Each Month Every month we identify the next highest-leverage automation: review request sequences, job follow-up flows, missed call recovery, appointment reminders, or custom reporting. The system gets smarter the longer we work together.
  • CAIO Access — Whatever's Next Hiring systems, new automations, custom dashboards, SOP documentation — bring the bottleneck and we build the solution. No scope ceiling, no "that's extra."
09 — The Exit Gap

Where you are. Where you want to be.

Bear Door Today
$1M+
annual revenue

Exit multiple: 1–2× revenue
Exit value: ~$1M – $2M
→
$1M–$3M
difference
Bear Door at Exit
$1M+
same revenue
Systemized, documented asset
Exit multiple: 3–5× revenue
Exit value: ~$3M – $5M
What's keeping Bear Door on the left side
✗
No documented SOPs — PE buyers can't verify operations run without Michael. If he's the answer to every question, the multiple drops.
✗
No KPI dashboards — Phil can't show buyers lead source performance, job type margins, or time-to-cash. What can't be measured can't be valued.
✗
Michael is the system — every new lead, every phone call, every scheduling decision runs through one person. That's a liability, not an asset.
✗
No scalable playbook — Pittsburgh is an idea, not a system. Buyers pay for businesses that replicate, not ones that depend on the founder to expand.

Our systems resolve each of these directly. SOPs get documented. KPI dashboards go live. AI handles incoming leads. The playbook gets written before Pittsburgh opens. That's the bridge from left to right — and it's the difference that shows up in the exit price.

11 — Why Disruptors Media

What makes us the right team for this.

01

You talk to Kimball. Every call.

No account managers, no handoffs, no "let me check with the team." Every strategy call, every build decision — direct line to the person doing the work.

02

We build infrastructure, not deliverables.

Agencies give you reports. We give you systems that compound. Each month builds on the last — the whole gets more valuable over time, not less.

03

AI moves fast. We move with it.

When the landscape shifts — and it will — we adapt your systems. You don't rebuild from scratch, pay to migrate, or start over. We adjust and keep moving.

12 — Next Steps

Here's what happens next.

  1. 1

    We talk Monday, June 1st at 6:30pm ET

    Already on the calendar. Read this with Phil over the weekend. Come with every question — the harder the better. That's what the call is for.

  2. 2

    If it makes sense, we move

    A 1-page agreement. No surprises in the fine print, nothing buried. Read it in five minutes. Proceed when you're ready — no pressure, no expiry games.

  3. 3

    Kick-off within 48 hours of signing

    We map the GHL build, get access to what we need, and start building in Week 1. No "onboarding month." No runway. We get to work immediately.
